andrea-vance-stuff.name: Andrea Vance

connections.role: United Future leader and Revenue Minister — Textgate 2013 (86 emails in 13 days; Dunne resigned; Vance's phone records and parliamentary swipe card data were improperly accessed)

facts.text: 'Textgate' (2013): exchanged 86 emails over 13 days with Revenue Minister Peter Dunne about GCSB leak inquiry; Dunne resigned; a subsequent inquiry improperly accessed Vance's phone records AND parliamentary swipe card movement data — a major NZ privacy scandal

andrea-vance-stuff.bio: Andrea Vance is a Northern Irish political journalist currently serving as a senior journalist at Stuff in Wellington. She grew up near Belfast, where her father edited three local newspapers. She worked for seven years as an investigative reporter at the British tabloid News of the World. When the phone hacking scandal broke, Vance was in the Glasgow newsroom and has denied any personal involvement, stating "no one had any idea it was going on." She subsequently worked as night news editor at The Scotsman before emigrating to New Zealand with her Kiwi husband. She became a prominent press gallery journalist, working for Fairfax/Stuff and later 1News. In 2013, she was at the centre of the Peter Dunne email scandal, in which minister Dunne resigned after refusing to release 86 email exchanges with Vance regarding a leaked GCSB report. Her phone records and parliamentary swipe-card data were improperly provided to the inquiry without her knowledge, prompting a major privacy breach complaint. She was named Political Journalist of the Year in 2025.
andrea-vance-stuff.methodology: Based on 20 articles scored by AI

audrey-young-nzherald.name: Audrey Young
audrey-young-nzherald.bio: Audrey Young is one of New Zealand's most experienced political journalists. She began her career as a sub-editor at the Auckland Sun, and after its closure in 1988 joined the New Zealand Herald as a sub-editor. She rose through the ranks to become the Herald's political editor, a role she held for 18 years (2003-2021), leading the paper's press gallery team in Wellington. In 2021, she transitioned to the role of Senior Political Correspondent, allowing her to focus on in-depth reporting. She was named Political Journalist of the Year at the Voyager Media Awards in 2018, 2020, and 2023. Her 30th anniversary at the Herald was acknowledged in Parliament. She has a particular interest in foreign affairs and Treaty of Waitangi issues, and served for six years as the union representative on the Press Council.

claire-trevett-nzherald.name: Claire Trevett

connections.role: Chief Media and Communications Adviser (from May 2025 — revolving door from NZ Herald Political Editor)

facts.text: Left NZ Herald in May 2025 after almost two decades as Political Editor to become Chief Media and Communications Adviser to the NZ Police Commissioner

claire-trevett-nzherald.bio: Claire Trevett served as the NZ Herald's political editor for several years, becoming one of the most recognised press gallery journalists in New Zealand. Based in Wellington, she was known for her sharp political analysis, witty writing style, and coverage of multiple election cycles and changes of government. Trevett left the Herald in May 2025 after almost two decades as Political Editor to become Chief Media and Communications Adviser for New Zealand Police.

derek-cheng-nzherald.name: Derek Cheng
derek-cheng-nzherald.bio: Derek Cheng is a senior political journalist at the New Zealand Herald. He graduated with a journalism diploma from Massey University in 2004 and joined the Herald shortly after, where he has worked for two decades. He has served multiple stints in the parliamentary press gallery and held the role of deputy political editor. His political reporting career spans the Helen Clark, John Key, and Jacinda Ardern eras. He is known for evidence-based political analysis. Outside journalism, Cheng is an accomplished mountaineer and adventure writer.

guyon-espiner-rnz.name: Guyon Espiner

connections.role: Public broadcaster funded by NZ Government
Former personal assistant to Labour MP Darren Hughes (married 2012 separated 2023)
Labour MP — Espiner's wife Emma Wehipeihana was his personal assistant

facts.text: Political journalist Duncan Garner served as best man at Espiner's 2012 wedding to Emma Wehipeihana

guyon-espiner-rnz.bio: Guyon Espiner (born 31 December 1970, Christchurch) is an investigative journalist at Radio New Zealand with over 30 years in print, television, and radio. After a decade in newspapers, including as political editor of the Sunday Star-Times, he joined TVNZ in 2003, becoming political editor in 2006 and anchoring Q+A, Agenda, and Breakfast. He moved to TV3's 60 Minutes in 2011, then joined RNZ in 2014 as co-host of Morning Report. Since 2019 he has produced acclaimed podcasts (The Ninth Floor, The Service, Red Line) and documentaries. He authored The Drinking Game. Espiner married Emma Wehipeihana in 2012; they separated in 2023. Wehipeihana had previously worked as a personal assistant to Labour MP Darren Hughes, who served as MC at their wedding.
guyon-espiner-rnz.methodology: Based on 25 articles scored by AI

henry-cooke-stuff.name: Henry Cooke

facts.text: Left journalism to work in public relations in London for several years before returning as deputy political reporter at The Post in September 2025; runs the 'Museum Street' politics newsletter

henry-cooke-stuff.bio: Henry Cooke is a political journalist at Stuff/The Post. He studied international relations, political science, and media studies at Victoria University of Wellington. He joined Stuff through a summer internship, moving to the press gallery in 2017. He rose to chief political reporter and deputy political editor. He left journalism to work in public relations in London before returning as deputy political reporter at The Post in September 2025. He runs the 'Museum Street' politics newsletter on Substack.

jane-patterson-rnz.name: Jane Patterson

connections.role: Public broadcaster funded by NZ Government

jane-patterson-rnz.bio: Jane Patterson is a senior journalist at Radio New Zealand who joined in May 2000. She spent most of her career in the Parliamentary Press Gallery, where she is a life member, including eight years as RNZ's political editor. She was appointed Director of Editorial Quality and Training at RNZ. Her awards include Best Coverage of a News Event for Election 2017 at the 2018 Voyager Awards.

jason-walls-nzherald.name: Jason Walls

facts.text: Moved from NZ Herald to Newstalk ZB Political Editor then to TVNZ 1News Business Correspondent (replacing Katie Bradford)

jason-walls-nzherald.bio: Jason Walls is a New Zealand political and business journalist currently serving as business correspondent at TVNZ/1News. He holds degrees in economics and journalism from AUT. He began at NBR and Interest.co.nz before joining the NZ Herald's press gallery team in 2018, rising to political editor. He moved to Newstalk ZB in 2021, becoming political editor in 2023. Walls served as Chair of the Parliamentary Press Gallery. His wife, Jacinta Gulasekharam, co-founded Dignity NZ, a social enterprise combating period poverty.

jo-moir.name: Jo Moir
jo-moir.bio: Jo Moir is the political editor at Radio New Zealand, a role she took up in early 2024. She completed her postgraduate journalism diploma at Massey University in 2006. She worked at the Wairarapa Times-Age, Taranaki Daily News, and the Dominion Post before joining Stuff's press gallery team in 2015 and then RNZ in 2018. She subsequently served as political editor of Newsroom from 2021 before returning to RNZ. She has reported on five prime ministers and covered three general elections.

john-campbell.name: John Campbell

connections.role: Public broadcaster funded by NZ Government

facts.text: Chief Correspondent at TVNZ/1News; announced in late 2025 to join RNZ Morning Report in 2026

john-campbell.bio: John Campbell (born 10 February 1964) is one of New Zealand's most recognised broadcast journalists. He began at Radio New Zealand as a business reporter in 1989. He moved to TV3 in 1991, co-anchoring 3 News (1998-2005). He fronted Campbell Live (2005-2015), whose cancellation prompted public outcry. He returned to RNZ in 2016 to host Checkpoint, then joined TVNZ in 2018. In December 2025 he was announced as co-host of RNZ's Morning Report from April 2026. He is married to documentary editor Emma Patterson.

katie-bradford-1news.bio: Katie Bradford is a veteran New Zealand political and business journalist with over 20 years' experience. She is the daughter of former Green Party MP and activist Sue Bradford and activist Jim Bradford. She spent more than a decade at TVNZ, working primarily as 1News business correspondent. In April 2025, Bradford left TVNZ to join Infrastructure New Zealand as strategy and engagement manager.

thomas-coughlan-nzherald.name: Thomas Coughlan
thomas-coughlan-nzherald.bio: Thomas Coughlan is the political editor of the New Zealand Herald. He holds a Master's degree in Political Thought and Intellectual History from the University of Cambridge (2014-2015). Coughlan joined the Parliamentary Press Gallery in 2018, working first for Newsroom and Stuff before moving to the NZ Herald in 2021. He is regarded as one of the younger generation's most authoritative political journalists.
